summary:
Brazil's Supreme Court sentenced Eduardo Bolsonaro, the third son of former President Jair Bolsonaro, to four years in prison for pressuring US officials to impose economic sanctions on Brazil. The court found that Eduardo aimed to influence his father's trial — the elder Bolsonaro is currently serving a 27-year sentence for a failed coup attempt in 2022. The ruling marks a significant blow to the Bolsonaro political family, which has faced mounting legal challenges since losing power.

At the G7 summit in France, leaders praised President Trump for securing a preliminary agreement with Iran, calling it a "breakthrough" achieved under his "strong leadership." The diplomatic shift comes as the group has been courting Trump at the summit. Meanwhile, the Ebola crisis in the Democratic Republic of Congo is worsening: Africa CDC warned that tens of thousands of contacts are not being traced, risking an outbreak that could surpass the 2014-2016 epidemic, which killed over 11,000 people. In Ukraine, Kyiv's "logistics lockdown" — scores of attacks on supply routes — has caused gasoline shortages, aiming to choke Russian forces in Crimea.

UK Prime Minister Keir Starmer condemned Russia for firing warning shots from a warship at a yacht outside British territorial waters, calling the action reckless. In Sri Lanka, Yoshitha Rajapaksa, son of former President Mahinda Rajapaksa, was arrested on corruption charges for allegedly using state funds for overseas training as a naval officer without proper qualifications. Israel launched fresh strikes on Lebanon, drawing criticism from President Trump, who said Prime Minister Netanyahu needed "to be more responsible with respect to Lebanon."

South Korea eased civilian restrictions on 270 square kilometers near the North Korean border, responding to farmer complaints and aiming to boost tourism. In Australia, One Nation's treasury spokesman Barnaby Joyce proposed cutting worker entitlements to help small businesses cope with inflation and high power prices. Bahrain stripped 69 people, including children, of their citizenship amid the war with Iran, accusing them of disloyalty and leaving them stateless.

headlines.title: Brazil Supreme Court sentences Bolsonaro's son to four years over US lobbying
headlines.summary: Eduardo Bolsonaro, son of former President Jair Bolsonaro, received a four-year prison sentence for pressuring US officials to impose sanctions on Brazil, aiming to influence his father's coup trial.

headlines.title: G7 leaders praise Trump for U.S.-Iran deal at France summit
headlines.summary: Group of 7 leaders at a summit in France called the preliminary U.S.-Iran agreement a breakthrough secured under President Trump's strong leadership, marking a diplomatic shift.

headlines.title: DR Congo Ebola outbreak could become worst in history as thousands untraced
headlines.summary: Africa CDC warns tens of thousands of Ebola contacts in DR Congo are not being traced, risking an outbreak surpassing the 2014-2016 epidemic that killed over 11,000 people.

headlines.title: Ukraine targets Russian supply routes to Crimea in logistics lockdown
headlines.summary: Ukraine conducts scores of attacks on supply routes to Crimea, causing gasoline shortages as part of a logistics lockdown to choke Russian forces.

headlines.title: UK Prime Minister Starmer calls Russian warship warning shots in Channel reckless
headlines.summary: Uk Prime Minister Keir Starmer condemned Russia's warning shots fired from a warship at a yacht outside UK territorial waters, calling the action reckless.

headlines.title: Sri Lanka arrests son of former President Mahinda Rajapaksa on corruption charges
headlines.summary: Yoshitha Rajapaksa, 38, was arrested for allegedly using state funds for overseas training as a naval officer without proper qualifications, targeting the once-powerful political clan.

headlines.title: Israel launches fresh strikes on Lebanon despite Trump criticism
headlines.summary: Israel conducted new strikes on Lebanon, with President Trump criticizing Prime Minister Netanyahu for not being more responsible, escalating regional tensions.

headlines.title: South Korea eases civilian curbs along border with North Korea
headlines.summary: South Korea announces easing of civilian restrictions on 270 sq km near North Korean border, responding to farmer complaints and aiming to boost local tourism and development.

framings.angle: Emphasized resident welcome and freer farming activities
Focused on the security buffer zone reduction and farmer complaints

headlines.title: One Nation proposes cutting worker entitlements to aid struggling small businesses
headlines.summary: One Nation's treasury spokesman Barnaby Joyce says the party is prepared to cut worker entitlements to help small businesses cope with inflation and high power prices.

headlines.title: Bahrain revokes citizenship of 69 people, rendering them stateless amid Iran war
headlines.summary: Bahrain strips 69 individuals, including children, of citizenship for alleged disloyalty during the war with Iran, leaving them stateless and facing potential expulsion.
